name: soapclient
title: SOAP Client
description: Invoke a SOAP 1.1/1.2 service with optional WSDL-driven operation discovery, auto soapAction injection, and request-body skeleton generation.

display.description: Invoke a SOAP service with WSDL-driven operation discovery and automatic request skeleton generation

settings.name: wsdlSourceType
settings.description: Choose how to provide the WSDL: browse a local file or enter a remote HTTP/HTTPS URL.

display.name: WSDL Source
display.description: Select WSDL File to browse a local file, or WSDL URL to enter a remote address.

settings.name: wsdlUrl
settings.description: Local WSDL file (selected via file browser).

display.name: WSDL File
display.description: Browse to select a local .wsdl file

settings.name: wsdlHttpUrl
settings.description: HTTP or HTTPS URL of the WSDL document (e.g. https://host/service?wsdl). NOTE: Browser CORS policy prevents the designer from fetching external URLs — operations and schema will NOT auto-populate. Use "WSDL File" for design-time features. The URL is used by the runtime (Go) only.

display.name: WSDL URL
display.description: Remote WSDL URL — design-time WSDL parsing is blocked by browser CORS. Use WSDL File for operation discovery.

settings.name: wsdlOperation
settings.description: Name of the WSDL operation to invoke. Required when a WSDL URL is set. The dropdown is populated automatically after the WSDL is fetched and parsed.

display.name: WSDL Operation
display.description: Select an operation from the parsed WSDL. Populated automatically when a valid WSDL URL or file is provided.

settings.name: autoUseWsdlEndpoint
settings.description: When enabled, the SOAP Service Endpoint setting is overridden with the endpoint declared in the WSDL <service> element.

display.name: Use WSDL Service Endpoint
display.description: Override the endpoint below with the one declared in the WSDL <service> element

settings.name: soapServiceEndpoint
settings.description: The endpoint URL of the SOAP service to invoke. When autoUseWsdlEndpoint is enabled this is overridden with the value from the WSDL <service> element.

settings.name: xmlMode
settings.description: When enabled, the request body is a JSON object whose keys map directly to XML elements. Use @-prefixed keys for XML attributes (e.g. @xmlns). The activity converts the JSON object to XML and places it inside the SOAP envelope without any WSDL-driven wrapping. The response is returned as a raw XML string. When disabled (default), the activity performs automatic JSON↔XML conversion and, when a WSDL operation is selected, wraps the body with the correct root element and namespace.

settings.name: xmlAttributePrefix
settings.description: Attribute prefix for JSON ↔ XML conversion. Default is '-'. Only used when XML Mode is disabled.

settings.name: authorization
settings.description: Enable HTTP authentication using an HTTP auth connector. When enabled select the authentication connection below.

settings.name: authorizationConn
settings.description: HTTP authentication connection used to inject Authorization headers.

settings.name: enableTLS
settings.description: Enable a secure TLS connection. When enabled the certificate fields below become available.

settings.name: skipTlsVerify
settings.description: Disable TLS certificate verification. WARNING: enables man-in-the-middle attacks. Use only for development or when connecting to services with self-signed certificates that cannot be added to the system trust store.

display.name: Skip TLS Verification
display.description: WARNING: disables certificate validation — development use only.

display.name: Server / CA Certificate
display.description: CA or server certificate file for TLS verification (.crt / .pem / .cer / .der)

display.name: Client Certificate
display.description: Client certificate file for mutual TLS (.crt / .pem / .cer / .der)

display.name: Client Private Key
display.description: Client private key file for mutual TLS (.key / .pem / .der)

settings.name: maxResponseBodyMB
settings.description: Maximum response body size in megabytes. Responses larger than this limit are rejected with an error. Default is 10 MB when set to 0.

display.name: Max Response Body (MB)
display.description: Reject responses larger than this size to prevent out-of-memory conditions.

settings.name: maxConnsPerHost
settings.description: Maximum number of simultaneous active HTTP connections to the SOAP endpoint host. Default is 100 when set to 0. Increase for high-concurrency flows that send many parallel SOAP calls to the same endpoint.

display.name: Max Connections Per Host
display.description: Cap on simultaneous connections to the SOAP host. Increase for high-concurrency deployments.

input.name: soapAction
input.description: Overrides the SOAPAction HTTP header. When a WSDL operation is configured and this field is empty the value is injected automatically from the WSDL binding.

input.name: httpQueryParams
input.description: Key-value pairs appended to the endpoint URL as query parameters. Merged with any existing query string from the endpoint.

input.name: soapRequestHeaders
input.description: SOAP <Header> element contents. JSON object (JSON mode) or JSON object / raw XML string (XML mode). Leave empty when no SOAP headers are required.

input.name: soapRequestBody
input.description: SOAP <Body> element contents. In JSON mode: a JSON object converted to XML automatically. In XML mode: a JSON object (use @-prefixed keys for XML attributes, e.g. @xmlns) or a raw XML string for exact element order control.

output.name: httpStatus
output.description: HTTP status code returned by the SOAP service.

output.name: isFault
output.description: True when the response contained a SOAP Fault.

output.name: soapResponsePayload
output.description: Successful SOAP response body (JSON object or XML string depending on XML Mode). When a WSDL operation is configured the wsdlResponseJSONSchema output describes the field structure.

output.name: soapResponseFault
output.description: SOAP Fault details when isFault is true. Structure follows the SOAP 1.1 Fault shape (faultcode, faultstring, faultactor, detail). The wsdlFaultJSONSchema output contains the exact JSON Schema.
